info.title: Threads API
info.description: OpenAPI source of truth for the Threads HTTP API. All registered routes are represented; schemas marked x-schema-status: placeholder need follow-up precision.

servers.description: Combined production app (replace with your instance API base)

post.description: Authenticate with username and password. Returns the user record without password_hash and sets the session cookie.

200.description: Authenticated user and Set-Cookie session header.

post.description: Delete the current session token when present and clear the session cookie.
Change the authenticated user password after validating the current password.
Create a human or bot user with an initial password. Authorized by the authenticated user's admin flag.

get.description: Owner-scoped event WebSocket upgrade endpoint. One socket carries events across all of the authenticated user's channels; payloads are the channel event schemas defined in api/openapi/ws-events.yaml, each enriched with channelId and a room object. HTTP upgrade is represented here.

200.description: Non-upgrade request response; websocket clients should expect 101 on successful upgrade.

get.description: WebSocket upgrade endpoint. HTTP upgrade is represented here; event payloads are defined in api/openapi/ws-events.yaml.

200.description: Non-upgrade request response; websocket clients should expect 101 on successful upgrade.

get.description: WebSocket upgrade endpoint. HTTP upgrade is represented here; event payloads are defined in api/openapi/ws-events.yaml.

200.description: Non-upgrade request response; websocket clients should expect 101 on successful upgrade.

get.description: Return online/offline presence for a comma-separated userIds query parameter via the presence Durable Object.
Return a previously uploaded R2 object. Dangerous stored content types are served as application/octet-stream.

post.description: Upload one multipart file field named file to R2 and create an unattached attachment record.

Transcribe a short audio clip (voice memo) to text with Workers AI Whisper. The request body is raw audio bytes (audio/webm, audio/mp4, audio/ogg); Whisper detects the container. Returns the transcription text, which the client drops into the message composer.

text.description: The transcribed text; empty when no speech was detected.

get.description: Fetch a safe HTTP(S) URL preview or return a cached preview. Returns preview: null when no usable preview is available.
Return the authenticated user profile used by the client session.

patch.description: Update mutable authenticated-user profile preferences. At least one recognized field is required.

get.description: Return exactly three frequently used reaction emojis, backfilled with defaults when needed.

post.description: Create a scoped API bearer token for the authenticated user. Restricted to admin users. The token is returned once and cannot be retrieved later. Scopes default to read/write and expiry defaults to 90 days.

get.description: List the authenticated user's API tokens. Restricted to admin users. Returns non-secret metadata including scopes and expiry — never the token value.

delete.description: Revoke one of the authenticated user's own API tokens by its hashed id (from GET /users/me/api-tokens). Restricted to admin users and scoped to the caller, so a user can only revoke their own tokens.

get.description: List webhook registrations owned by the authenticated user. Bot agents should call this with the bot's bearer token to inspect the bot's own delivery URLs. Secrets are never returned after creation.

200.description: Webhook registrations for the authenticated user.

post.description: Register one webhook URL for the authenticated user. Bot agents should call this with the bot's bearer token. The signing secret is returned once and cannot be retrieved later.

201.description: Created webhook registration with one-time signing secret.

delete.description: Delete one webhook registration owned by the authenticated user. Deleting and recreating a webhook is the rotation path for the signing secret.

post.description: Create an API bearer token for another user by id (for example, a bot the admin just created via POST /users). Restricted to admin users (the session user's is_admin flag), authorized by the caller's session/bearer credential. The token is returned once and cannot be retrieved later.
Allow bot users to advertise their own model and DM capabilities. Non-bot users receive 403.

get.description: Search users by username or display name. Optional channel filters out existing channel members.

parameters.name: q
parameters.description: Case-insensitive username/display-name search. Empty or omitted returns users up to the server limit.

parameters.name: channel
parameters.description: When provided, existing members of this channel are excluded from results.

get.description: List all bot users (role='bot'). Used to populate the per-user ephemeral-channel bot picker.
List visible direct message channels for the authenticated user, including partner details, unread state, and sendability metadata.

post.description: Create or reopen a direct message channel with another user. Existing DMs return 200; newly-created DMs return 201.

put.description: Persist the authenticated user's custom ordering for visible direct message channels.

patch.description: Hide a direct message channel for the authenticated user without deleting the conversation.

get.description: List non-DM, non-archived channels the authenticated user belongs to, including notification tier and unread metadata.

post.description: Create a channel and add the authenticated user as its first member.

get.description: List public non-DM channels, including whether the authenticated user is a current member and each channel member count.
Get channel metadata by id. Private channels are only visible to current members.

patch.description: Update mutable channel metadata and settings for a channel the authenticated user belongs to.

delete.description: Delete a channel and its related data. Only the channel creator or an admin may delete it.

post.description: Join a public channel, or rejoin one that the authenticated user previously left.
Hard-remove the authenticated user from a channel membership.

get.description: List current channel members. Private channel member lists are only visible to current members.

post.description: Add a user to a channel by user id or username. The caller must be a current member.

delete.description: Remove a member from a channel. Non-admin users may only remove themselves.
Soft-leave a non-DM channel, hiding it from the user while preserving channel history.

patch.description: Update the authenticated user's notification tier for a channel membership.

get.description: List channel folders for the authenticated user, including ordered channel ids assigned to each folder.

post.description: Create a channel folder for the authenticated user at the next folder position.

patch.description: Update the authenticated user's folder name, position, and/or collapsed state.

delete.description: Delete one of the authenticated user's folders. Channel records are not deleted.

post.description: Move a channel into one of the authenticated user's folders, optionally at an explicit position.

delete.description: Remove a channel from one of the authenticated user's folders.

put.description: Bulk update folder positions and/or channel-folder item positions for the authenticated user.

get.description: List non-thread messages in a channel. Supports cursor pagination, fetching newer messages, and anchoring around a message id.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Return messages older than this message id.

parameters.name: around
parameters.description: Return a window centered around this message id.

parameters.name: after
parameters.description: Return messages newer than this message id.

post.description: Send a top-level message to a channel.
Mark the latest visible message in a channel as read for the current user.

get.description: Get a single message by id after verifying channel membership.

patch.description: Edit a message owned by the current user.

delete.description: Soft-delete a message owned by the current user.

post.description: Mark a top-level message as resolved. The thread dims in place rather than being deleted, distinguishing threads the author is finished with from ones still needing work. Any channel member may resolve, which also lets a bot resolve on a user's behalf via an API token.

delete.description: Clear the resolved state of a message so its thread is no longer dimmed.

put.summary: Set or rename a thread title
put.description: Set a human-readable title on a message thread. The id may identify either the top-level message or one of its replies; the title is stored on the thread root. Titles preserve case and punctuation and do not need to be unique. Any current channel member may rename the thread. Automatic callers can request set-if-empty behavior so they do not overwrite an existing title.

get.description: List replies in a message thread.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Return replies after this message id.

parameters.name: around
parameters.description: Return a window centered around this reply id.

parameters.name: after
parameters.description: Return newer replies after this message id.

parameters.name: before
parameters.description: Return replies before this message id. Used with latest-first thread views to page older replies.

post.description: Send a reply to a message thread.
Add a reaction to a message as the current user. Creating a new reaction returns 201; adding the same emoji again is idempotent and returns 200.

delete.description: Remove the current user's reaction emoji from a message. The emoji path segment is URL-decoded by the API.

post.description: Attach or update process status and token usage metadata for a message, then broadcast the process_status event to channel subscribers.
Reset a failed, killed, or restarted message process back to queued, clear its process error text, and broadcast process_status and process_retry events.

get.description: List the latest messages that mention the authenticated user across channels they belong to.
Search messages visible to the authenticated user. Supports FTS query syntax plus channel, sender, date, and attachment/reaction/link filters.

parameters.name: q
parameters.description: Full-text search query. Supports quoted phrases, uppercase OR, -term exclusions, and term* prefix matching. Empty query is allowed when at least one filter is provided.

parameters.name: channel
parameters.description: Restrict results to a channel id.

parameters.name: from
parameters.description: Restrict results to messages sent by this user id.

parameters.name: since
parameters.description: Unix timestamp lower bound for message created_at. Invalid or negative values are ignored.

parameters.name: until
parameters.description: Unix timestamp upper bound for message created_at. Invalid or negative values are ignored.

parameters.name: has
parameters.description: Comma-separated content filters. Recognized values: link, reaction, file.

get.description: Return the public sandboxed HTML runtime for a widget iframe. The parentOrigin query parameter must match an allowed Threads client origin.

parameters.name: channelId
parameters.description: Channel id exposed to widget code via ThreadsWidget.getContext().

parameters.name: parentOrigin
parameters.description: Embedding Threads client origin; must be allowlisted.

post.description: Create a private ephemeral channel for the authenticated user, optionally adding the Tela bot as the auto-responder when that bot account exists.
Soft-archive an ephemeral channel so it disappears from the sidebar while preserving history and searchability. Only the creator or an admin may archive.

delete.description: Clear the archive timestamp for an ephemeral channel. The caller must still be a channel member.

post.description: Set the inferred slug for an ephemeral channel that has not already been auto-named. The slug is sanitized server-side and collisions are disambiguated.
Promote an ephemeral channel into a first-class channel while preserving its id, members, history, and metadata in place.
Clear auto_named_at for an ephemeral channel so the next message can trigger the agent-loop name inference again. Only the creator or an admin may regenerate.

get.description: List pinned messages for a channel visible to the authenticated member, newest pin first.

post.description: Pin an existing message in the channel. The message must belong to the channel and not already be pinned.

delete.description: Remove a pin for a message in the channel.

get.description: Return the kanban board for a channel with its cards ordered by position.

post.description: Create a kanban board for the channel using the server default columns. Fails with 409 if one already exists.

put.description: Replace the kanban board column key list for the channel.

post.description: Create a card on the channel board at the next position in the selected column.

put.description: Update mutable fields for a kanban card. Moving to in-progress may emit a channel message for bot processing.

delete.description: Delete a kanban card and its activity log.

get.description: List activity entries for a kanban card, newest first.
Return all registered widgets without their executable code, ordered newest first.

post.description: Create a widget owned by the authenticated user. name and code are required; icon defaults to PuzzlePiece.

get.description: Return one widget, including its executable code.

put.description: Update fields on a widget. Only the widget creator can update it, and at least one field is required.

delete.description: Delete a widget and remove it from all channels. Only the widget creator can delete it.

get.description: Return widgets added to a channel. Private channels require membership.

200.description: Channel widgets ordered by most recently added.

post.description: Add an existing widget to a channel. Private channels require membership; duplicate adds are ignored.

delete.description: Remove a widget from a channel. Private channels require membership.

get.summary: Read all user values for a widget KV key
get.description: Return all per-user values for a widget KV key. This endpoint is restricted to bot users.

200.description: Global widget KV values for the key.

get.description: Return the authenticated user's value for a widget-scoped key.

put.description: Create or replace the authenticated user's value for a widget-scoped key. Values are serialized as JSON.

delete.description: Delete the authenticated user's value for a widget-scoped key. The response is successful even if the key did not exist.

get.description: Return the VAPID public key the client uses to create browser push subscriptions.

post.description: Register or update the authenticated user's browser push subscription and enable push notifications.

delete.description: Remove one browser push subscription for the authenticated user by endpoint.

get.description: Return the authenticated user's push notification preferences, defaulting to enabled and all notifications when no row exists.

put.description: Upsert the authenticated user's push notification preferences.

get.description: List sync-state rows whose keys start with the required prefix query parameter.

parameters.name: prefix
parameters.description: Key prefix to match. SQL LIKE wildcards are escaped before querying.

200.description: Sync-state entries matching the requested prefix.

get.description: Return one sync-state row by exact key.

put.description: Create or update one sync-state value by key.

get.description: Returns channel IDs where the authenticated user has non-empty draft text or draft attachments.

200.description: Channels that currently have a draft for the authenticated user.

get.description: Returns the authenticated user’s draft text and attachments for a channel. Missing drafts return empty content and no attachments.

200.description: Draft content and attachments for the channel.

put.description: Creates, replaces, or clears the authenticated user’s draft for a channel. Empty content with no attachment IDs clears the draft.

get.summary: List saved drafts for a channel
get.description: Returns saved drafts owned by the authenticated user in the requested channel, newest first. Requires channel membership.

200.description: Saved drafts for the authenticated user in this channel.

post.description: Persists non-empty draft content for the authenticated user in the requested channel. Requires channel membership.

delete.description: Deletes a saved draft owned by the authenticated user. Drafts owned by other users are treated as not found.

patch.summary: Schedule or unschedule a saved draft
patch.description: Sets a saved draft delivery time to a future ISO timestamp, or clears scheduling with null. Only the draft owner can schedule it.

200.description: Updated saved draft with the new scheduled_at value.

get.description: Lists agent process runs visible to the caller (channel members see their channels' runs; admins see all). Optionally filtered by status. Stale running runs are swept to killed on read.

post.description: Called by a bot when it begins a turn, to register a run that the management UI can track and cancel. The run is identified by its own id (there is no OS pid). Bot principals only.

201.description: The created process id and status.

bot_id.description: Owning bot id. Bot callers may only use their own id; admins may create a process on a bot's behalf.

post.description: Called once by a bot at startup, before it consumes new events. Marks all running or queued processes owned by the authenticated bot as restarted, updates their triggering messages, and broadcasts the terminal state. Bot principals only; this operation assumes one active runtime per bot identity.
Cancels every running or queued process the caller can see (admins: all). Each cancellation marks the run killed and signals the owning agent over WebSocket and webhook.

200.description: How many runs were cancelled and their ids.

get.description: Returns a single process run. Requires channel membership (admins: any).

patch.description: Called by the owning bot to advance status (running/done/error) and report cumulative metrics (tool/reply counts, token usage). Bot principals only.

delete.description: Cooperatively cancels a running or queued process: marks it killed and signals the owning agent over WebSocket and webhook so it can stop. Any channel member (or admin) may cancel.

post.description: Called by the owning bot to increment a tool-call/reply counter or add token usage as a turn progresses. Bot principals only.
Reset another user's password by id. Restricted to admin users (the session user's is_admin flag).

delete.description: Revoke another user's API token by its hashed id. Restricted to admin users (the session user's is_admin flag).
Delete a user by id. Restricted to admin users (the session user's is_admin flag).

get.description: List the 50 most recent server error-log rows. Restricted to admin users (the session user's is_admin flag).

delete.description: Permanently delete this entire workspace (tenant) and all of its Cloudflare infrastructure. Owner-only: the caller must be the workspace owner, and must confirm by typing the exact workspace name and re-entering their password. Irreversible — removes data for every user in the workspace. The teardown itself runs asynchronously in the provisioner.

confirm.description: Must exactly equal the workspace name (TENANT_NAME).

password.description: The owner's current password, re-entered to authorize the deletion.

post.description: Threads sends this HTTPS POST to each active webhook URL owned by a bot user when that bot would receive the same channel event over WebSocket. Delivery is at-least-once; receivers must verify the signature, handle duplicates by delivery id, and reply with any 2xx status to acknowledge.

parameters.name: X-Threads-Event-Id
parameters.description: Stable event id for this webhook delivery. Use this id for idempotency.

parameters.name: X-Threads-Timestamp
parameters.description: Unix epoch seconds used when computing the signature.

parameters.name: X-Threads-Signature
parameters.description: HMAC SHA-256 signature of '<timestamp>.<raw request body>' with the webhook secret, formatted as 'sha256=<hex>'.

200.description: Any 2xx response acknowledges delivery. Non-2xx responses and timeouts are retried within the delivery budget.

sessionCookie.name: session
sessionCookie.description: Browser session cookie set by POST /auth/login. Generated non-browser clients should prefer bearerAuth when they have a user API token.

bearerAuth.description: User API token sent as Authorization: Bearer <token>. This is the preferred authentication mode for generated TS/Python clients and Hermes integration.

threadsWebhookSignature.name: X-Threads-Signature
threadsWebhookSignature.description: Webhook receiver verification header. The value is HMAC SHA-256 of '<timestamp>.<raw request body>' with the webhook secret, formatted as 'sha256=<hex>'.

Error.description: Standard JSON error envelope returned by documented 4xx/5xx responses. Generated clients expose this as a normal parsed response for documented error statuses; truly undocumented statuses should be handled via the generator's unexpected-status mechanism.

error.description: Human-readable error message. Callers should branch on HTTP status codes, not parse this string.

PlaceholderData.description: Placeholder response schema until the operation is modeled precisely.

UserRecord.description: Authenticated user record returned by login. password_hash is intentionally excluded.

bot_capabilities_json.description: Raw JSON string stored for bot capability metadata.

ephemeral_bot_id.description: Bot user auto-added to ephemeral channels this user creates (null = none).

resolved_at.description: Unix timestamp when the thread was resolved.

resolved_by.description: User id that resolved the thread.

applied.description: Whether this request changed the stored title.

thread_id.description: Top-level message id that identifies the thread.

thread_title_updated_at.description: Unix timestamp when the title was last changed.

displayName.description: Trimmed server-side and truncated to 100 characters.

nameColor.description: Empty string is also accepted by the API to clear the color.

ephemeralBotId.description: Bot user id auto-added to ephemeral channels this user creates. Must reference an existing bot user; null (or empty string) clears it (no bot auto-added).

FrequentEmojisResponse.description: Exactly three frequently used reaction emojis for the current user, backfilled with defaults.

userId.description: User id of the reacting user.

username.description: Username of the reacting user, or null if unavailable.

MessageReaction.description: A reaction attached to a message or thread reply.

Message.description: Message read model. The API preserves legacy snake_case DB fields and exposes camelCase aliases for newer clients.

thread_title.description: Optional human-readable title stored on a top-level message thread.

idempotencyKey.description: Optional client-generated key. Re-sending with the same key returns the already-created message (200) instead of creating a duplicate.

title.description: Human-readable title; case and punctuation are preserved.

if_unset.description: When true, set the title only if the thread is currently untitled.

emoji.description: Reaction emoji or emoji token to add for the current user.

collapsed.description: SQLite boolean flag; 1 when collapsed, 0 when expanded.

channels.description: Channel ids assigned to the folder, ordered by folder item position.

is_dm.description: SQLite boolean flag; DMs always return 1.

has_unread.description: SQLite boolean flag in list responses; omitted or null when creating a DM.

Channel.description: Channel database read model. The API currently returns legacy snake_case fields.

is_private.description: SQLite boolean flag; 1 for true, 0 for false.

is_dm.description: SQLite boolean flag; 1 for true, 0 for false.

is_ephemeral.description: SQLite boolean flag; 1 for true, 0 for false.

board_enabled.description: SQLite boolean flag; 1 for true, 0 for false.

has_unread.description: SQLite boolean flag; 1 for true, 0 for false.

is_member.description: SQLite boolean flag; 1 for true, 0 for false.

board_enabled.description: SQLite boolean flag accepted by the API; use 1 for true and 0 for false.

snippet.description: FTS snippet with sentinel markers around matches, or null for filter-only searches.

bm25_score.description: FTS bm25 score. Filter-only searches return 0.

SearchMessage.description: Search result row: message columns plus joined user/channel fields and optional FTS ranking/snippet fields.

messageId.description: Message ID to pin in the channel.

created_at.description: Unix timestamp when the pin was created.

pin_created_at.description: Unix timestamp when the pin was created.

message_created_at.description: Unix timestamp when the pinned message was created.

KanbanBoard.description: Raw kanban board record. columns is the JSON-encoded column key array stored by the API.

columns.description: JSON-encoded array of column keys, e.g. ["todo","in-progress","review","done"].

KanbanCard.description: Raw kanban card record returned by board endpoints. metadata is stored as a JSON string when present.

priority.description: Server defaults to normal; existing data may contain custom priority strings.

PresenceMapResponse.description: Map of requested user ids to online status.

ProcessRecord.description: Process read model returned by the process monitor APIs. Includes raw DB fields plus joined user/channel/bot display fields when available.

resolved_at.description: Unix seconds when the linked top-level thread was marked resolved. For processes attached to thread replies, this is inherited from the thread root.

resolved_by.description: User id that marked the linked top-level thread resolved.

started_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp, normalized from SQLite datetime when needed.

bot_id.description: Owning bot id. Bot callers may only use their own id; admins may create a process on a bot's behalf.

processId.description: Process identifier associated with the message.

error_text.description: Optional process error text; server caps stored and broadcast value to 4000 characters.

WebhookUrl.description: Webhook receiver URL. Must use https and a public hostname; credentials, localhost, private IPs, and internal hostnames are rejected.

WebhookEventType.description: Event type from the embedded payload. Values match the WebSocket ServerEvent `type` values documented in api/openapi/ws-events.yaml.

WebhookEventData.description: The event payload exactly as it is sent on channel WebSockets. See `ServerEvent` in api/openapi/ws-events.yaml for the complete per-type schemas.

WebhookDelivery.description: Signed event delivery sent by Threads to a registered webhook URL.

event_id.description: Stable id across retries. Store this id to make webhook handling idempotent.

timestamp.description: Unix epoch seconds when the delivery body was created.

bot_user_id.description: Bot user id this delivery targets, so one URL can serve multiple bot identities.

channel_id.description: Channel or DM id that produced the event, so one webhook URL can handle all rooms for a bot.

last_delivered_at.description: Unix epoch seconds for the most recent successful delivery, or null when none has succeeded.

token_id.description: Stable SHA-256 token id for the API token this webhook belongs to.

secret.description: Webhook signing secret returned once at creation. Store it securely; later list responses never include it.

token_id.description: Optional SHA-256 token id to attach the webhook to when authenticated by session. Bearer-authenticated requests infer the current token.

scopes.description: Token permissions. Omitted defaults to both read and write.

expires_in_days.description: Lifetime in days. Omitted defaults to 90; null explicitly disables expiry.

webhook_urls.description: Optional webhook URLs to register for the token owner at token creation time. Created webhook secrets are returned once in the token creation response.

id.description: Stable SHA-256 token id for later token-scoped operations.

expires_at.description: Unix epoch seconds when the token expires, or null for no expiry.

webhooks.description: Webhook registrations created from `webhook_urls`, including one-time signing secrets. Omitted when no webhooks were requested.

id.description: Stable, non-secret identifier (SHA-256 of the token value). Used to revoke the token.

expires_at.description: Unix epoch seconds when the token expires, or null for no expiry.

created_at.description: Unix epoch seconds when the token was created.

EphemeralChannelResponse.description: Ephemeral channel create response. Shape matches the channel read model with ephemeral-specific flag values.

slug.description: Free-form slug candidate; sanitized to a short kebab-case channel name server-side.

name.description: Optional replacement name before promotion; sanitized server-side.

archived_at.description: Unix timestamp when the channel was archived.

auto_named_at.description: Unix timestamp when the channel was auto-named.

JsonValue.description: Any JSON value accepted or returned by widget data endpoints. Kept non-recursive for OpenAPI client-generator compatibility.

PlaceholderSuccess.description: Successful response. Schema is intentionally broad until this operation is modeled precisely.

PresenceMap.description: Presence state keyed by user id.

LinkPreviewResponse.description: Fetched or cached link preview; preview is null when unavailable.
